Kaindy Lake Kazakhstan: A Travel Guide to Kaindy Lake, Charyn Canyon & Essential Tips
Welcome to our beautiful end-of-summer getaway to the 9th largest country in the world — Kazakhstan . Much like Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan...

Welcome to our beautiful end-of-summer getaway to the **9th largest country in the world — Kazakhstan**. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-1.jpg)

Much like Kyrgyzstan, **Kazakhstan** is filled with dazzling alpine lakes, breathtaking treks, and plenty of rustic adventures. The best part? No visa hassle anymore — the **visa is FREE and on arrival for Indian passport holders**.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-2.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-4.jpg)

This was a trip I had initially planned with Kyrgyzstan, but visa complications and border closures delayed it. Determined to make it work, we picked up exactly where we left off — in the untamed heart of **Central Asia**.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-6.jpg)

**Kazakhstan Travel Itinerary**

**  4-Day Plan for Nature Lovers & Hikers**

- Land in Almaty, visit **Charyn Canyon**
Land in Almaty, visit **Charyn Canyon**

- Day trip to **Kaindy Lake Kazakhstan**
Day trip to **Kaindy Lake Kazakhstan**

- Hike **Kolsai Lake 1 & 2**
Hike **Kolsai Lake 1 & 2**

- Explore **Almaty city** and its stunning gardens
Explore **Almaty city** and its stunning gardens

- [FAQ](/post/saotomeandprincipe#viewer-7c3nb)
[FAQ](/post/saotomeandprincipe#viewer-7c3nb)

- [Guide Information](#viewer-dlkqh) 
[Guide Information](#viewer-dlkqh) 

- [Costs](#viewer-beacp)
[Costs](#viewer-beacp)

- [Highlights](#viewer-36b8a)
[Highlights](#viewer-36b8a)

- [Final Thoughts](#viewer-a4vo1)
[Final Thoughts](#viewer-a4vo1)

**Flights + Visa**

We flew directly with **FlyDubai Kazakhstan**, and the flight was short — just 4 hours.

For Indian passport holders, the **Kazakhstan visa on arrival** was simple and **completely free**. The immigration process was fast; we were only asked about the duration of our stay.

**Solo Travel in Kazakhstan**

**Kazakhstan solo travel** is absolutely worth it. Even in our short time there, the people were welcoming, the streets felt safe, and the country offered a beautiful balance of nature and history. As always, stay vigilant and use common sense, but solo female travelers should feel fairly comfortable here.

**Best Time to Visit Kazakhstan**

While summer (June-July) is popular, the **best time to visit Kazakhstan** is **August to September**. The hiking trails are accessible, the heat is bearable, and the autumn colors begin to show — adding extra charm to every hike.

**Language & Local Guide Info**

Outside of **Almaty**, English is rarely spoken. In the mountain villages, having a **local guide** is essential. Our guide handled everything smoothly, and if you need recommendations — drop me a message, I’d be happy to share his contact.

** Kazakhstan Travel Cost**

The **Kazakhstan travel cost** is similar to [Kyrgyzstan](/post/how-to-make-the-most-of-7-days-in-kyrgyzstan) — very budget-friendly. However, due to fewer tourists, prices can feel slightly higher in rural areas. Still, it’s far more affordable than Western destinations for the kind of raw natural beauty it offers.

**Currency & Travel Insurance**

Use **local Kazakhstani Tenge (KZT)** for tips and small purchases. Travel insurance is recommended — we used our usual provider, but many prefer options like World Nomads.

**Rent a Car or Local Guide?**

If you’re planning a month-long road trip or camping experience, renting a car could be fun. But unless you're fluent in **Russian or Kazakh**, it’s safer to go with a **local guide**. Roads can be rough, especially in the Southeast where most natural wonders lie.

**Vegan + Vegetarian Food in Kazakhstan**

This was the only challenge. Unlike Kyrgyzstan, the **villages in Kazakhstan** are less tourist-friendly in terms of food. Vegetarian options were limited, but we made do with chai, fruits, and the occasional fresh vegan momo (thanks to a kind homestay chef!).


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-7.jpg)

**Where We Stayed – Almaty City Guide**

In **Almaty**, we stayed at the centrally located [Ambassador Hotel](https://www.booking.com/hotel/kz/ambassador.html?chal_t=1757059212452&force_referer=https%3A%2F%2Fmanage.wix.com%2F), perfect for exploring on foot. Almaty has a relaxed **Eastern-European vibe**, colorful gardens, food trucks, and live street music. If you’re planning a short stay, **Almaty travel** is ideal as a base.

**Kazakhstan Highlights: Canyons, Lakes & Mountains**

**Charyn Canyon Kazakhstan**

Often called the **“Mini Grand Canyon”**, **Charyn Canyon** surprised us with its shifting colors — from red to greenish-black. We hiked both the upper and lower loops.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-8.jpg)

 The best part? At the end of the lower trail, a tranquil river awaits tired feet.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/atriptokazakhstan-9.jpg)

Welcome to our beautiful end-of-summer getaway to the 9th largest country in the world — Kazakhstan.

Much like Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan is filled with dazzling alpine lakes, breathtaking treks, and plenty of rustic adventures. The best part? No visa hassle anymore — the visa is FREE and on arrival for Indian passport holders.

This was a trip I had initially planned with Kyrgyzstan, but visa complications and border closures delayed it. Determined to make it work, we picked up exactly where we left off — in the untamed heart of Central Asia.

Kazakhstan Travel Itinerary

** 4-Day Plan for Nature Lovers & Hikers**

  • Land in Almaty, visit Charyn Canyon Land in Almaty, visit Charyn Canyon

  • Day trip to Kaindy Lake Kazakhstan Day trip to Kaindy Lake Kazakhstan

  • Hike Kolsai Lake 1 & 2 Hike Kolsai Lake 1 & 2

  • Explore Almaty city and its stunning gardens Explore Almaty city and its stunning gardens

  • FAQ FAQ

  • Guide Information Guide Information

  • Costs Costs

  • Highlights Highlights

  • Final Thoughts Final Thoughts

Flights + Visa

We flew directly with FlyDubai Kazakhstan, and the flight was short — just 4 hours.

For Indian passport holders, the Kazakhstan visa on arrival was simple and completely free. The immigration process was fast; we were only asked about the duration of our stay.

Solo Travel in Kazakhstan

Kazakhstan solo travel is absolutely worth it. Even in our short time there, the people were welcoming, the streets felt safe, and the country offered a beautiful balance of nature and history. As always, stay vigilant and use common sense, but solo female travelers should feel fairly comfortable here.

Best Time to Visit Kazakhstan

While summer (June-July) is popular, the best time to visit Kazakhstan is August to September. The hiking trails are accessible, the heat is bearable, and the autumn colors begin to show — adding extra charm to every hike.

Language & Local Guide Info

Outside of Almaty, English is rarely spoken. In the mountain villages, having a local guide is essential. Our guide handled everything smoothly, and if you need recommendations — drop me a message, I’d be happy to share his contact.

** Kazakhstan Travel Cost**

The Kazakhstan travel cost is similar to Kyrgyzstan — very budget-friendly. However, due to fewer tourists, prices can feel slightly higher in rural areas. Still, it’s far more affordable than Western destinations for the kind of raw natural beauty it offers.

Currency & Travel Insurance

Use local Kazakhstani Tenge (KZT) for tips and small purchases. Travel insurance is recommended — we used our usual provider, but many prefer options like World Nomads.

Rent a Car or Local Guide?

If you’re planning a month-long road trip or camping experience, renting a car could be fun. But unless you’re fluent in Russian or Kazakh, it’s safer to go with a local guide. Roads can be rough, especially in the Southeast where most natural wonders lie.

Vegan + Vegetarian Food in Kazakhstan

This was the only challenge. Unlike Kyrgyzstan, the villages in Kazakhstan are less tourist-friendly in terms of food. Vegetarian options were limited, but we made do with chai, fruits, and the occasional fresh vegan momo (thanks to a kind homestay chef!).

Where We Stayed – Almaty City Guide

In Almaty, we stayed at the centrally located Ambassador Hotel, perfect for exploring on foot. Almaty has a relaxed Eastern-European vibe, colorful gardens, food trucks, and live street music. If you’re planning a short stay, Almaty travel is ideal as a base.

Kazakhstan Highlights: Canyons, Lakes & Mountains

Charyn Canyon Kazakhstan

Often called the “Mini Grand Canyon”, Charyn Canyon surprised us with its shifting colors — from red to greenish-black. We hiked both the upper and lower loops.

 The best part? At the end of the lower trail, a tranquil river awaits tired feet.

Tips for visiting Charyn Canyon:

  • Arrive early to beat the heat Arrive early to beat the heat

  • Bring sunblock & water Bring sunblock & water

  • Small entrance fee applies Small entrance fee applies

  • Join a group tour from Almaty to save costs Join a group tour from Almaty to save costs

Kaindy Lake Kazakhstan – A Sunken Forest

Kaindy Lake is surreal — a turquoise glacial lake with tree trunks rising out of the water. Caused by a 1911 earthquake, it’s easily one of the most unique lakes in the world.

Top tips:

  • Combine with Kolsai Lake on a 2-day trip Combine with Kolsai Lake on a 2-day trip

  • Roads are rough — hire a local guide Roads are rough — hire a local guide

  • Carry your passport — border checks are common Carry your passport — border checks are common

  • Visit in late afternoon for golden light reflections Visit in late afternoon for golden light reflections

Kolsai Lake – Jewel of Tien Shan Mountains

We stayed at Saty village, a rustic homestay area that reminded me of Kyrgyz yurts.

Kolsai Lake — also called the “Pearl of the Tien Shan” — features blue-green reflections surrounded by alpine forests and meadows.

There are three lakes in total; we hiked from the first to the second — a 9 km one-way trail, not for the faint-hearted. We also saw travelers on horseback navigating the steep terrain.

Kolsai Lake Tips:

  • Hike requires stamina; horses are an alternative Hike requires stamina; horses are an alternative

  • Stay overnight at Saty village Stay overnight at Saty village

  • Bring packed food — limited availability Bring packed food — limited availability

  • Guides are highly recommended due to poor connectivity Guides are highly recommended due to poor connectivity

Almaty – Where Nature Meets City

Almaty charmed us with its wide avenues, laid-back cafés, and parks bursting with color.

With Russian-influenced architecture, it felt more polished than Bishkek.

We had no itinerary — just hours of strolling and soaking in Fall hues.

Final Thoughts

Whether you’re into rugged treks or chilled city walks, Kazakhstan has something for everyone. It’s got glacier lakes, dramatic canyons, historic cities, and jaw-dropping hikes — all at a backpacker-friendly cost.

The Tien Shan mountains, Kaindy Lake, and Charyn Canyon make this a dream for hikers and photographers alike.

If you’re an adventurous traveler seeking offbeat Central Asia, Kazakhstan travel guide should be your go-to. And if you’ve got more time — combine this with Uzbekistan or Kyrgyzstan for a 2-3 week Central Asia travel itinerary.

**Quito travel guide**  

Find amazing things to do in Ecuador with our itinerary for must-visit places and helpful tips for four action-packed days in the country (beyond just the Galápagos Islands).


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-1.jpeg)

From the Cotopaxi volcano rising above the clouds to the turquoise waters of Quilotoa, Ecuador is a land of contrasts where history, adventure, and natural beauty come together. Whether you love culture, hiking, or food, this Ecuador itinerary has the perfect balance.


### **ITINERARY QUITO TRAVEL GUIDE**
Day 1 - Quito Guayasamin Museum, La Caroline Parque, TelefériQ, Church of El San Fransisco 

Day 2 - Quilotoa Crater Lagoon

Day 3 - Old Town and Cultural Center Quito

Must visit: Plaza Granda, Archbishop/Presidential Palace, Plaza San Fransisco and the La Iglesia de la Compania de Jesus, El Panecillo

Day 4 - Cotopaxi Volcano 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-2.jpeg)

Over the summer, we visited the continent to do a bit of country hopping. When we first planned this trip, a lot of my friends were extremely surprised that we chose to visit Ecuador…well, that actually surprised me. Ecuador is a stunning country with amazing culture, history and food. Plus, there is the visa on arrival. There was no way I was passing on this place. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-3.jpeg)

While Quito has enough to keep you occupied for days I would recommend venturing out to the neighboring towns on day trips to get your adrenaline pumping. All the major attractions are just within reach and there will be no shortage of things to do. 

**Pro Tip:** when I plan an itinerary, I like to balance our activities between adventure and laid back (since I prefer to be on the move but Mr. A likes his down time). This itinerary alternates between a busy day and chill time within the city. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-4.jpeg)

Ecuador is so much more than the Galapagos islands. There is plenty you can do without spending a fortune going back and forth from the Islands. From trekking up active volcanoes to zip-lining through cloud forests, Ecuador has something for everyone. 

**Best Time to Visit Ecuador**

- **June – September**: Best weather with mild temperatures.
**June – September**: Best weather with mild temperatures.

- **July**: Peak tourist season (school holidays).
**July**: Peak tourist season (school holidays).

- **December**: Rainy season, best avoided.

**Visa Tip:** Ecuador offers **Visa on Arrival for Indians** (valid up to 90 days). Carry your return ticket for immigration.

![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-17.jpeg)

This was the perfect spot to catch a break (you know, with all that Indian food) and avoid the afternoon sun.  


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-18.jpeg)

We continued walking up a steep hill and along winding streets with beautiful views of the city below. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-19.jpg)

The streets are super funky with colorful houses all the way up.. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-20.jpeg)

After a 20 minute walk, you will reach the **Guayasamin Museum**. Oswaldo Guayasamin was a legendary artist, well known all over the world for his artistic talent and quirky Colombian collection of artifacts. His stunning house has been converted into a museum to house all of these collections (unfortunately, you're not allowed to click pictures on the inside). 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-21.jpeg)

You can easily spend few hours here and enjoy the mind blowing views right from his balcony. I can only imagine how amazing he felt with the early morning light streaming through the windows. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-22.jpeg)

Our last stop for the day was **Church of El San Fransisco**. This is a 16th century roman catholic church. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-23.jpeg)

**Noteworthy mention: **
If you have the time, I would also recommend going to the **TelefériQ. **This is one of the highest cable car lifts in the world (rises up to an altitude of around 2.5 miles). We skipped this since we did the cable car in Bogota, Colombia. 


No trip to Ecuador is complete without a trek to **Quilotoa Lagoon**, just 3 hours from Quito. This volcanic crater lake is famous for its mesmerizing blue-green waters caused by dissolved minerals.

- **Scenic bus ride** with views of the Andean highlands.
**Scenic bus ride** with views of the Andean highlands.

- **Trek down to the lagoon** – a steep path that’s fun but challenging.
**Trek down to the lagoon** – a steep path that’s fun but challenging.

- **Trek back up** – prepare for a workout (or hire a mule).
**Trek back up** – prepare for a workout (or hire a mule).

The views are worth every step.

📍 More info:[ ](https://www.ecuador.com/attractions/quilotoa-lagoon)[Quilotoa Lagoon](https://www.ecuador.com/attractions/quilotoa-lagoon)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-24.jpeg)

You will board the bus at 8 a.m. and reach Quilotoa around 12 with a few stops along the way. The drive up is just as scenic.  


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-25.jpeg)

Try not to sleep through it.. or you will miss the gorgeous green mountains and cute villages! 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-26.jpeg)

The tour includes few light munches - fruits, cookies, chips and water followed by lunch. Your first stop will be just outside off the lake - the Quilotoa Crater Rim. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-27.jpeg)

You will have amazing panoramic views of the crater. This will be your first sighting, so there is a good chance you won't want to leave and continue staring in awe at color of the water. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-28.jpg)

but nope...get ready to run (or roll) down a steep path. The mud is extremely light with tiny bits of rocks. 

**Pro tip: **make sure you are wearing proper hiking shoes with good grip for this hike. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-29.jpg)

You will slip a fair few times but that's part of the fun (as long as you are careful not to slip on mule dung). Jokes apart, enjoy the trip down because the trek back up is going to be extremely tiring. There will be mules that can carry back up if you are exhausted. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-30.jpeg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-31.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-32.jpeg)

It's all worth it. The lake is absolutely fascinating. We went on a sunny day, so the lake was a stunning shade of blue and green. It would be difficult to describe the feeling of seeing this place in the flesh. ** **


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-33.jpeg)

Dissolved minerals in the water create the deep blue-green color in the lake. You can see the water turn different colors at different times during the day. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-34.jpg)

The mountains tower above the lake and the clear sky above reflect in the deep waters below to create a blue that would be hard to describe. I especially loved the pretty lavender flowers. The entire place was dreamy. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-35.jpeg)

We spent close to an hour taking pictures in every direction...before making the 40 minute trek back up. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-cover.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-37.jpeg)

You will stop for a traditional lunch on the way back - soup, corn, cheese, salad and rice.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-38.jpeg)

You will be back in Quito by 7. You will have the best views to dream about that night. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-39.jpeg)

Old Town Quito is bustling with energy, history, and charm. Did you know it was the **first city in the world declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site**?


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-40.jpeg)

**Must-Visit Places:**

- **Plaza Grande** – Independence Square with the Archbishop & Presidential Palaces.
**Plaza Grande** – Independence Square with the Archbishop & Presidential Palaces.

- **La Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús** – A gold-covered masterpiece of baroque design.
**La Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús** – A gold-covered masterpiece of baroque design.

- **Plaza San Francisco** – Lively square with street vendors and cafés.
**Plaza San Francisco** – Lively square with street vendors and cafés.

- **El Panecillo** – Hilltop with a 40m tall Virgin of Quito statue overlooking the city.
**El Panecillo** – Hilltop with a 40m tall Virgin of Quito statue overlooking the city.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-41.jpeg)

Take the hop-on-hop-off bus for an easy city tour or wander through the colorful colonial streets at your own pace.

Learn more:[ ](https://ecuador.travel/)[Ecuador Tourism Board](https://ecuador.travel/)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-42.jpg)

Did you know Quito was the world's first city to be declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site? 


The** Independence Square** or the Plaza Grande in Old town is culturally significant with plenty of historic plazas and churches. This is where you will find plenty of historic plazas and ancient churches. There are key monuments all around the square. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-43.jpeg)

The bus will take you to all the main spots around the independence square, so you can just jump off at the **La Compania de Jesus church** and spend some time exploring the interiors of this 17th century church. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-44.jpeg)

It is actually covered with gold and is the largest gothic structure in the continent with cool Ecuadorian animal inspired gargoyles. It's a must visit in the city. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-45.jpeg)

Your next stop will be the **Archbishop palace**. Get off at this place and walk around for few hours before getting your next bus back (the buses pass by once every hour). 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-46.jpeg)

Followed by the **Quito's Cathedral. **The entrance is free. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-47.jpeg)

Next head on to **Plaza San Francisco** (yes that is what it's called). This is where you will find street vendors, amazing food, cute corner cafes and much more. Take your time admiring the colonial streets as you wander on. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-48.jpeg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-49.jpeg)

After lunch, get the bus to the top of the hill - also the last stop on the bus. **El Panecillo. **From here you will have some of the best views of the city. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-50.jpeg)

What amazed me was that this is a land of beautiful contrasts. It’s a sprawling metropolis, yet you’re never too far away from serene Andean mountain peaks.  


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-51.jpg)

The **El Panecillo** is actually an iconic location with a 40 meter tall aluminum mosaic statue of La Virgen de Quito (Virgin of Quito) sitting on top of the hill. The statue is adorned with a crown of stars, wings and she appears to be chained to a crocodile (I actually thought it was a dragon). 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-52.jpeg)

You can see the busy & sprawling metropolis, yet you’re never too far away from serene Andean mountain peaks. On a clear day, you can even see the Cotopaxi Mountain from up here. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-53.jpg)

Our last stop for the day was Cumbaya valley. A quaint suburban 30 minutes away. Had the best hot chocolate, gelato and more beautiful views. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-54.jpeg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-55.jpeg)

**Noteworthy mentions: **

**Itchimbía**

I really wanted to go here but it was all the way across town and a long bus ride away. This park is famous for its big orange Quito Sign against the mountains. As I researched more about it, the place seemed like a great place to hang out and relax after a long day out. This place might be worth checking out if you want that the gram' worthy picture. 


You will hear a lot of hype about **La Mitad del Mindo** - this is where the equator passes through Ecuador. Do I think it's worth a visit? A visit to del Mindo is a half day activity. It's out of the way from the city and it will be a struggle to club with a city tour. 


If you have more than a few days in the country and want to explore every inch of Quito - yes, it's worth a visit. If you are looking to maximize your time in the city, give this one a miss. 


On the fourth and last day of our trip, we took another day trip to the Cotopaxi Volcano. Again, you will find plenty of tours doing trips from Quito. We went with a tour company that was recommended by our hotel. The trip is about two hours from the city.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-56.jpeg)

This snow-capped beauty is one of the tallest active volcanoes in the world and is the 2nd highest point in Ecuador. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-57.jpeg)

Don't worry - you will drive up on dirt roads and eventually you will reach the mouth of the crater. That's where you hike begins. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-58.jpeg)


### **Day 4 – Cotopaxi Volcano Adventure**

End your Ecuador trip with a bang by hiking **Cotopaxi**, one of the world’s tallest active volcanoes. Just two hours from Quito, this snow-capped beauty dominates the landscape.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-59.jpeg)


### **Hike from the parking lot** to the refuge at 4,800m.

- Enjoy a warm drink at base camp.
Enjoy a warm drink at base camp.

- On clear days, you can see Cotopaxi even from Quito.
On clear days, you can see Cotopaxi even from Quito.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-60.jpeg)

Once you reach the base camp, you can enjoy a hot chocolate or a coffee at the cafe. 

The summit is 5,897 meters (that's an elevation of about 3.6 miles). We really wanted to do the summit but we didn’t have the time. If we ever decide to visit Ecuador again, we’ll definitely head back here. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-61.jpg)

Did you know Cotopaxi is one of the few glaciers located on the equator? The experience of climbing its slopes, battling the wind, and soaking in the views is unforgettable.

Learn more:[ ](https://www.parquesnacionales.gob.ec/cotopaxi)[Cotopaxi National Park](https://www.parquesnacionales.gob.ec/cotopaxi)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-62.jpeg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-63.jpeg)

Post your climb, you will drive back towards the city for lunch en route Quito. Catch the sunset on your way back, grab dinner and catch a good night's sleep! 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-64.jpeg)

Ecuador surprised us with its incredible diversity. From Quito’s historic plazas to Cotopaxi’s volcanic slopes and Quilotoa’s dazzling lagoon, this small country packs in huge adventures.

We usually don’t linger in cities, but Quito truly impressed us with its mix of culture, food, and mountain views. Ecuador has yet to be spoiled by mass tourism, making it a perfect destination for curious travelers.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-65.jpg)

More inspiration:[ ](https://www.lonelyplanet.com/ecuador)[Lonely Planet Ecuador Guide](https://www.lonelyplanet.com/ecuador)

If South America is on your bucket list, don’t just think Peru or Colombia – give Ecuador a chance, and it will reward you in unexpected ways. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-66.jpg)

This country is teeming with goodness, and has yet to be spoiled by an onslaught of superficial tourism. Ever since we visited Peru, we have wanted to be back. With its stunning tapestry of landscapes, islands, coasts, culture and wildlife, South America is one gorgeous dream.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/an-amazing-four-day-travel-itinerary-and-guide-to-the-beautiful-quitotravelguide-67.jpg)

Stay tuned for more adventures from **Colombia and Costa Rica.**

Quito travel guide

Find amazing things to do in Ecuador with our itinerary for must-visit places and helpful tips for four action-packed days in the country (beyond just the Galápagos Islands).

From the Cotopaxi volcano rising above the clouds to the turquoise waters of Quilotoa, Ecuador is a land of contrasts where history, adventure, and natural beauty come together. Whether you love culture, hiking, or food, this Ecuador itinerary has the perfect balance.

ITINERARY QUITO TRAVEL GUIDE

Day 1 - Quito Guayasamin Museum, La Caroline Parque, TelefériQ, Church of El San Fransisco

Day 2 - Quilotoa Crater Lagoon

Day 3 - Old Town and Cultural Center Quito

Must visit: Plaza Granda, Archbishop/Presidential Palace, Plaza San Fransisco and the La Iglesia de la Compania de Jesus, El Panecillo

Day 4 - Cotopaxi Volcano

Over the summer, we visited the continent to do a bit of country hopping. When we first planned this trip, a lot of my friends were extremely surprised that we chose to visit Ecuador…well, that actually surprised me. Ecuador is a stunning country with amazing culture, history and food. Plus, there is the visa on arrival. There was no way I was passing on this place.

While Quito has enough to keep you occupied for days I would recommend venturing out to the neighboring towns on day trips to get your adrenaline pumping. All the major attractions are just within reach and there will be no shortage of things to do.

Pro Tip: when I plan an itinerary, I like to balance our activities between adventure and laid back (since I prefer to be on the move but Mr. A likes his down time). This itinerary alternates between a busy day and chill time within the city.

Ecuador is so much more than the Galapagos islands. There is plenty you can do without spending a fortune going back and forth from the Islands. From trekking up active volcanoes to zip-lining through cloud forests, Ecuador has something for everyone.

Best Time to Visit Ecuador

  • June – September: Best weather with mild temperatures. June – September: Best weather with mild temperatures.

  • July: Peak tourist season (school holidays). July: Peak tourist season (school holidays).

  • December: Rainy season, best avoided. December: Rainy season, best avoided.

We flew with Avianca and Copa Airlines – spacious, great service, and smooth connections across South America.

Visa Tip: Ecuador offers Visa on Arrival for Indians (valid up to 90 days). Carry your return ticket for immigration.

Spanish is the official language. A few useful words:

  • Hola (hello) Hola (hello)

  • Adiós (bye) Adiós (bye)

  • Cuánto (how much) Cuánto (how much)

  • Entrada (entrance) Entrada (entrance)

  • Salida (exit) Salida (exit)

Locals are friendly and will appreciate your effort to speak their language.

Where We Stayed – Hotel Casa Q, Quito

We stayed at Hotel Casa Q in New Town Quito.

Pros:

  • Spacious rooms with natural light. Spacious rooms with natural light.

  • Great breakfast spread with fresh bread, fruits, and yogurt. Great breakfast spread with fresh bread, fruits, and yogurt.

  • Cozy reading and games area. Cozy reading and games area.

  • Lovely little garden. Lovely little garden.

  • Cons: Cons:

  • Street noise at night (not ideal for light sleepers). Street noise at night (not ideal for light sleepers).

Pro Tip: Pre-book airport pickup through your hotel (around $25).

The room was not too big but it was spacious enough for the two of us. The room had plenty of light though. The breakfast spread was good with fresh bread, yogurt, fruits and pastries. The best part is that this place is just a stone’s throw away from the city where you will find cabs, buses, malls and plenty of great restaurants.

A word of caution: since this is right in the city, it got very loud during the night from activities on the street. I wouldn’t recommend this place if you are a light sleeper.

They also had a cozy recreational area with books and board games. I love to read books in buses, so this definitely got them few extra brownie points.

Don’t forget to check out the cute garden, which is the best place to hang out after a tiring day out. I was ready to move in here!

  • Cabs: $2–$3 within the city. Cabs: $2–$3 within the city.

  • Street food: $2–$5. Street food: $2–$5.

  • Day trips: $75–$100 (private tours slightly higher). Day trips: $75–$100 (private tours slightly higher).

Overall, Ecuador is one of the most affordable destinations in South America.

If you are planning to do day trips, you can book tours on Viator or Tripadvisor. I found this to be the easiest way to do Ecuador, hassle-free. You can also just walk to the city bus stop and book your own day tour or get the city hop-on hop-off bus tour within Quito. The tours range from $75 to $100 per person depending on whether you opt for a private or group tour.

Pro tip: If you have enough time, I would recommend doing the overnight stay at Cotopaxi and finishing at Quilotoa. While it is more expensive to do this, you can explore both places at your own pace and enjoy the stunning Andean landscape. It would be approximately $150 per person including transport, a guide and food for all 4 times.

We found Quito much safer than neighboring Colombia. People were polite and welcoming.

** Tips:**

  • Keep an eye on belongings. Keep an eye on belongings.

  • Carry essential documents. Carry essential documents.

  • Avoid carrying too much cash. Avoid carrying too much cash.

Traditional Ecuadorian food is hearty and flavorful. Some must-try dishes for vegetarians:

  • Locro de Papa – potato and cheese soup topped with avocado. Locro de Papa – potato and cheese soup topped with avocado.

  • Empanadas de Viento – deep-fried, cheese-stuffed bread. Empanadas de Viento – deep-fried, cheese-stuffed bread.

  • Llapingachos – potato pancakes with cheese and purple yam. Llapingachos – potato pancakes with cheese and purple yam.

Don’t miss Ecuadorian hot chocolate – perfect after a chilly mountain hike!

We did get the chance to try a few exciting local dishes with our dear Ecuadorian friends who took us out to an authentic restaurant smack in the middle of old town. The place and the ambience was just amazing.

Here are few things I recommend you try if you are vegetarian:

Locro de Papa - this is a delicious rich potato and cheese soup that is usually topped with avocado. This is a classic Andean soup meant to be had in the mountains to keep you warm on a cold night. I had this soup at least twice a day - it made for such a hearty meal.

Empanada di Viento - your basic cheese stuffed deep fried bread

Llapingachos - fried potato, pancakes stuffed with cheese and purple yam

  • Sunscreen, hat, sunglasses (for sunny days). Sunscreen, hat, sunglasses (for sunny days).

  • Light cardigan + rain jacket (weather can change quickly in the Andes). Light cardigan + rain jacket (weather can change quickly in the Andes).

  • Hiking shoes (essential for Quilotoa and Cotopaxi). Hiking shoes (essential for Quilotoa and Cotopaxi).

  • Travel insurance and basic medicines. Travel insurance and basic medicines.

Kick off your Ecuador itinerary in the capital city, Quito.

  • Guayasamín Museum – Home to the works of Ecuador’s most famous artist. Guayasamín Museum – Home to the works of Ecuador’s most famous artist.

  • La Carolina Park – A sprawling urban park perfect for a relaxed stroll. La Carolina Park – A sprawling urban park perfect for a relaxed stroll.

  • TelefériQo – One of the highest cable cars in the world (skip if you’ve done Bogotá). TelefériQo – One of the highest cable cars in the world (skip if you’ve done Bogotá).

  • Church of San Francisco – A stunning 16th-century Roman Catholic church. Church of San Francisco – A stunning 16th-century Roman Catholic church.

Walking through Quito’s colorful streets, you’ll come across the national slogan, Ecuador ama la vida (“love life”), which perfectly sums up the vibe here.

Learn more: Quito UNESCO World Heritage Site

A short while later you will come across a huge sign with Quito’s national slogan** Ecuador ama la vida, **which means “love life”. This, to me, pretty much summarizes the laid back attitude of the country.

As you stroll down the road, you will Walk through the La Carolina Park. This is a huge park with paddle-boats, bike paths, kid’s play area and more important, plenty of shade.

This was the perfect spot to catch a break (you know, with all that Indian food) and avoid the afternoon sun.

We continued walking up a steep hill and along winding streets with beautiful views of the city below.

The streets are super funky with colorful houses all the way up..

After a 20 minute walk, you will reach the Guayasamin Museum. Oswaldo Guayasamin was a legendary artist, well known all over the world for his artistic talent and quirky Colombian collection of artifacts. His stunning house has been converted into a museum to house all of these collections (unfortunately, you’re not allowed to click pictures on the inside).

You can easily spend few hours here and enjoy the mind blowing views right from his balcony. I can only imagine how amazing he felt with the early morning light streaming through the windows.

Our last stop for the day was Church of El San Fransisco. This is a 16th century roman catholic church.

**Noteworthy mention: ** If you have the time, I would also recommend going to the **TelefériQ. **This is one of the highest cable car lifts in the world (rises up to an altitude of around 2.5 miles). We skipped this since we did the cable car in Bogota, Colombia.

No trip to Ecuador is complete without a trek to Quilotoa Lagoon, just 3 hours from Quito. This volcanic crater lake is famous for its mesmerizing blue-green waters caused by dissolved minerals.

  • Scenic bus ride with views of the Andean highlands. Scenic bus ride with views of the Andean highlands.

  • Trek down to the lagoon – a steep path that’s fun but challenging. Trek down to the lagoon – a steep path that’s fun but challenging.

  • Trek back up – prepare for a workout (or hire a mule). Trek back up – prepare for a workout (or hire a mule).

The views are worth every step.

📍 More info: Quilotoa Lagoon

You will board the bus at 8 a.m. and reach Quilotoa around 12 with a few stops along the way. The drive up is just as scenic.

Try not to sleep through it.. or you will miss the gorgeous green mountains and cute villages!

The tour includes few light munches - fruits, cookies, chips and water followed by lunch. Your first stop will be just outside off the lake - the Quilotoa Crater Rim.

You will have amazing panoramic views of the crater. This will be your first sighting, so there is a good chance you won’t want to leave and continue staring in awe at color of the water.

but nope…get ready to run (or roll) down a steep path. The mud is extremely light with tiny bits of rocks.

**Pro tip: **make sure you are wearing proper hiking shoes with good grip for this hike.

You will slip a fair few times but that’s part of the fun (as long as you are careful not to slip on mule dung). Jokes apart, enjoy the trip down because the trek back up is going to be extremely tiring. There will be mules that can carry back up if you are exhausted.

It’s all worth it. The lake is absolutely fascinating. We went on a sunny day, so the lake was a stunning shade of blue and green. It would be difficult to describe the feeling of seeing this place in the flesh. ** **

Dissolved minerals in the water create the deep blue-green color in the lake. You can see the water turn different colors at different times during the day.

The mountains tower above the lake and the clear sky above reflect in the deep waters below to create a blue that would be hard to describe. I especially loved the pretty lavender flowers. The entire place was dreamy.

We spent close to an hour taking pictures in every direction…before making the 40 minute trek back up.

You will stop for a traditional lunch on the way back - soup, corn, cheese, salad and rice.

You will be back in Quito by 7. You will have the best views to dream about that night.

Old Town Quito is bustling with energy, history, and charm. Did you know it was the first city in the world declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site?

Must-Visit Places:

  • Plaza Grande – Independence Square with the Archbishop & Presidential Palaces. Plaza Grande – Independence Square with the Archbishop & Presidential Palaces.

  • La Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús – A gold-covered masterpiece of baroque design. La Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús – A gold-covered masterpiece of baroque design.

  • Plaza San Francisco – Lively square with street vendors and cafés. Plaza San Francisco – Lively square with street vendors and cafés.

  • El Panecillo – Hilltop with a 40m tall Virgin of Quito statue overlooking the city. El Panecillo – Hilltop with a 40m tall Virgin of Quito statue overlooking the city.

Take the hop-on-hop-off bus for an easy city tour or wander through the colorful colonial streets at your own pace.

Learn more: Ecuador Tourism Board

Did you know Quito was the world’s first city to be declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site?

The** Independence Square** or the Plaza Grande in Old town is culturally significant with plenty of historic plazas and churches. This is where you will find plenty of historic plazas and ancient churches. There are key monuments all around the square.

The bus will take you to all the main spots around the independence square, so you can just jump off at the La Compania de Jesus church and spend some time exploring the interiors of this 17th century church.

It is actually covered with gold and is the largest gothic structure in the continent with cool Ecuadorian animal inspired gargoyles. It’s a must visit in the city.

Your next stop will be the Archbishop palace. Get off at this place and walk around for few hours before getting your next bus back (the buses pass by once every hour).

Followed by the **Quito’s Cathedral. **The entrance is free.

Next head on to Plaza San Francisco (yes that is what it’s called). This is where you will find street vendors, amazing food, cute corner cafes and much more. Take your time admiring the colonial streets as you wander on.

After lunch, get the bus to the top of the hill - also the last stop on the bus. **El Panecillo. **From here you will have some of the best views of the city.

What amazed me was that this is a land of beautiful contrasts. It’s a sprawling metropolis, yet you’re never too far away from serene Andean mountain peaks.

The El Panecillo is actually an iconic location with a 40 meter tall aluminum mosaic statue of La Virgen de Quito (Virgin of Quito) sitting on top of the hill. The statue is adorned with a crown of stars, wings and she appears to be chained to a crocodile (I actually thought it was a dragon).

You can see the busy & sprawling metropolis, yet you’re never too far away from serene Andean mountain peaks. On a clear day, you can even see the Cotopaxi Mountain from up here.

Our last stop for the day was Cumbaya valley. A quaint suburban 30 minutes away. Had the best hot chocolate, gelato and more beautiful views.

**Noteworthy mentions: **

Itchimbía

I really wanted to go here but it was all the way across town and a long bus ride away. This park is famous for its big orange Quito Sign against the mountains. As I researched more about it, the place seemed like a great place to hang out and relax after a long day out. This place might be worth checking out if you want that the gram’ worthy picture.

You will hear a lot of hype about La Mitad del Mindo - this is where the equator passes through Ecuador. Do I think it’s worth a visit? A visit to del Mindo is a half day activity. It’s out of the way from the city and it will be a struggle to club with a city tour.

If you have more than a few days in the country and want to explore every inch of Quito - yes, it’s worth a visit. If you are looking to maximize your time in the city, give this one a miss.

On the fourth and last day of our trip, we took another day trip to the Cotopaxi Volcano. Again, you will find plenty of tours doing trips from Quito. We went with a tour company that was recommended by our hotel. The trip is about two hours from the city.

This snow-capped beauty is one of the tallest active volcanoes in the world and is the 2nd highest point in Ecuador.

Don’t worry - you will drive up on dirt roads and eventually you will reach the mouth of the crater. That’s where you hike begins.

Day 4 – Cotopaxi Volcano Adventure

End your Ecuador trip with a bang by hiking Cotopaxi, one of the world’s tallest active volcanoes. Just two hours from Quito, this snow-capped beauty dominates the landscape.

Hike from the parking lot to the refuge at 4,800m.

  • Enjoy a warm drink at base camp. Enjoy a warm drink at base camp.

  • On clear days, you can see Cotopaxi even from Quito. On clear days, you can see Cotopaxi even from Quito.

Once you reach the base camp, you can enjoy a hot chocolate or a coffee at the cafe.

The summit is 5,897 meters (that’s an elevation of about 3.6 miles). We really wanted to do the summit but we didn’t have the time. If we ever decide to visit Ecuador again, we’ll definitely head back here.

Did you know Cotopaxi is one of the few glaciers located on the equator? The experience of climbing its slopes, battling the wind, and soaking in the views is unforgettable.

Learn more: Cotopaxi National Park

Post your climb, you will drive back towards the city for lunch en route Quito. Catch the sunset on your way back, grab dinner and catch a good night’s sleep!

Ecuador surprised us with its incredible diversity. From Quito’s historic plazas to Cotopaxi’s volcanic slopes and Quilotoa’s dazzling lagoon, this small country packs in huge adventures.

We usually don’t linger in cities, but Quito truly impressed us with its mix of culture, food, and mountain views. Ecuador has yet to be spoiled by mass tourism, making it a perfect destination for curious travelers.

More inspiration: Lonely Planet Ecuador Guide

If South America is on your bucket list, don’t just think Peru or Colombia – give Ecuador a chance, and it will reward you in unexpected ways.

This country is teeming with goodness, and has yet to be spoiled by an onslaught of superficial tourism. Ever since we visited Peru, we have wanted to be back. With its stunning tapestry of landscapes, islands, coasts, culture and wildlife, South America is one gorgeous dream.

Stay tuned for more adventures from Colombia and Costa Rica.

Cheers,

 Anki

Our Last Trip of the Year: Exploring Victoria Falls Zambia
Our last trip of the year took us to yet another off the beaten track country in Africa. This time we landed in Zambia , only to be...

Our last trip of the year took us to yet another off the beaten track country in Africa. This time we landed in **Zambia**, only to be welcomed by a new variant of a pandemic that refuses to die out. That did not stop us from making a long trip from Lusaka to Livingstone to stay at not one but two fantastic properties right next to the mighty **Victoria Falls Zambia**.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-1.jpg)

Both these properties are located within the same compound inside the National Park, so in essence you have the best of both worlds within a few minutes of each other. Although you will soon find out that both places offer vastly different experiences.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-2.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-4.jpg)


### [Avani Victoria Falls](https://www.avanihotels.com/en/victoria-falls) 

**Avani Victoria Falls Resort – Where to Stay in Zambia**

We spent our first two nights at this three-star resort.

After an arduous 9-hour shared bus ride, it felt so good to be welcomed by smiling, dancing Zambians. The atmosphere was welcoming, the staff courteous and the relaxing vibe was just what we needed to forget the nightmare ensuing around the world for just a few days.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-6.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-8.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-18.jpg)


### **Safety and Hygiene**

There were sanitizers everywhere and all staff wore masks at restaurants and all common areas. The rooms are always well sanitized, which is especially reassuring if you are wondering, “Is Zambia safe for tourists?” 


### **Rooms**

Though this is rated as a three-star property, to us, it felt much more posh than that. Massive African art adorn the high-ceiling rooms and passages.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-12.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-14.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-16.jpg)

The rooms are done up tastefully with all the necessary amenities. There are 221 rooms and most of them either face the national park or the pool. Ours faced the pool. The bold orange all over the resort was a definite mood booster.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-18.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-20.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-22.jpg)

We were so excited to wake up to see the wildlife grazing right outside your balcony. Just your regular morning in wild Africa!


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-24.jpg)


### **Wildlife Encounters**

There are so many things to love about this property but most of all it is the wildlife roaming freely across the yard that was a breath of fresh air. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-25.jpg)

We found them everywhere. Imagine waking up to jumping monkeys... or vividly colored birds. You get all that and more. In spades.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-26.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-28.jpg)

We are still in awe of how wonderful it was to watch the zebras grazing peacefully as people quietly walked past. Not a care in the world. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-30.jpg)

**Dining**

You can choose between two restaurants. One of the restaurants is purely buffet-style, serving a generous breakfast spread every morning. From flavored yogurts, fresh fruits, bread, eggs to order, cold cuts, baked beans and more. Warm coffee to order. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-31.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-33.jpg)

For lunch and dinner, there is the Pool side Bar & Grill restaurant with an elaborate menu. I liked how many vegetarian options there were. We even found a few Indian appetizers on the menu. You will also have live entertainment at night where you will find local performers putting up a show. How cool is that? 

For lunch and dinner, there is the Poolside Bar & Grill restaurant with an elaborate menu. I liked how many vegetarian options there were. We even found a few Indian appetizers on the menu. You will also have live entertainment at night where local performers put up a show.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-35.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-37.jpg)


### **Special Zambian Dinner**

If you are celebrating a special occasion, you cannot miss indulging in a beautiful local dinner set up by the hote.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-39.jpg)

It is a four-course meal elegantly curated with soup, starters, mains and desserts - all from the vegetable-heavy **Zambian cuisine**.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-40.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-42.jpg)

We asked the chef to make it vegetarian friendly and we were surprised by how delicious every course tasted. The privacy, the candle-lit set up was just the date we needed after a long travel day.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-32.jpg)


### **Location**

The Avani property, and its sister hotel, are both located in the center of **Mosi-O-Tunya National Park**, making it the perfect location to take a stroll to watch the thunderous **Victoria Falls Zambia**.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-45.jpg)

The entrance to the hike of the Victoria Falls costs $120 per person and I highly recommend that it be one of the **things to do in Zambia** during your stay at Avani. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-46.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-48.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-50.jpg)


### **Royal Livingstone Anantara – Luxury Stay in Zambia**

If you love staying at luxury hotels around the world, this spectacular resort in southern Africa should be on your bucket list. The majestic view, the elegant upscale vibe, and the cool sophistication of the interiors is like taking a step back in time..  


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-52.jpg)

This is one of the best luxury properties in **Victoria Falls Zambia**, named after the famous David Livingstone himself.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-53.jpg)

The first thing that hits us as we make our way over to the property is the lush resort setting. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-54.jpg)


### **Safety and Hygiene**

Cannot go wrong with Anantara service, hospitality and hygiene anywhere in the world. Top of the line every time


### **The Lounge & The Travelers Bar**

This was one of my favorite corners on the property. As we were waiting for our rooms to be ready, we were welcomed with drinks and wet towels.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-6.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-57.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-59.jpg)

What drew me in was the colonial-style architecture, the old books, the gramophone — I half expected Winston Churchill to show up there.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-61.jpg)

Just beside the lounge is the Traveler’s Bar. If you are looking to rest your legs after a long **Zambia safari**, you cannot go wrong with this space.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-62.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-64.jpg)


### **Rooms**

Slightly more intimate than Avani, this resort has 173 rooms. Our deluxe room was spacious with ridiculously comfortable king-size beds and a balcony so big that it felt like a whole other room with breathtaking views of the **Zambezi River**.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-66.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-68.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-70.jpg)


### **Wildlife**

Just like Avani, the highlight of this property is the wildlife. We saw giraffes, zebras, impalas, and monkeys roaming freely. It was like having a safari inside the resort.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-70.jpg)


### **The Anantara Spa**

How could you not indulge in some pampering when you are in the midst of such beauty? The masseuse was pitch perfect and so were the views. That was followed by a floral bath.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-73.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-75.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-77.jpg)


### **Pool**

The pool is huge with extraordinary views of wildlife and the African bush, with the mist of **Victoria Falls Zambia** in the backdrop.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-79.jpg)


### **Dining Experiences**

- **Breakfast at the Restaurant**: Hot buffet, fresh salads, cut meats, sparkling wine, and fruits with a backdrop of lush gardens.
**Breakfast at the Restaurant**: Hot buffet, fresh salads, cut meats, sparkling wine, and fruits with a backdrop of lush gardens.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-80.jpg)


### **Breakfast on the Jetty**:


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-81.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-87.jpg)

For a special occasion, you can float along the **Zambezi River** while enjoying a decadent spread..

Would you look at these landscapes?


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-85.jpg)


### **Kubu Deck for Sundowners**: Watch the sunset over **Victoria Falls Zambia** with hippos in the distance.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-86.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-88.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-90.jpg)


### **The Drift **

For lunch and dinner, you have the Drift. I was pleasantly surprised to see a whole bunch of vegetarian and keto options. We had ourselves a beautiful date night under the moon-lit sky. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-92.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-94.jpg)


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-96.jpg)


### **Location **

Located just a few hundred meters from **Victoria Falls Zambia**, you can spot the mist of the mighty falls throughout the day. 


![Blog Image](/blog-images/avaniandroyallivingstoneanantaravictoriafallszambia-98.jpg)


### **Sustainability and Responsible Travel in Zambia**

From employing locals to supporting the villages to no single-use plastic, both properties impressed me with their sustainable measures.


### **Things to Do in Zambia – Adventure Beyond the Resorts**

No matter which of these properties you stay at, you are never far away from adventure.

You can indulge in adrenaline-packed activities ranging from hikes, zip-lining, bungee jumping to river rafting down the **Zambezi River**. For a slower pace, you can also enjoy a **Zambezi River cruise** or even a **walking safari Zambia** experience.

At Royal Livingstone, don’t miss giraffe feeding and the magical breakfast on the jetty overlooking **Victoria Falls Zambia**.

t miss giraffe the feeding and the breakfast on the jetty overlooking the Victoria Falls. 


### **Zambia Travel Guide – Which Property to Choose?**

So which one to pick?

The Avani is rated three-star while the Royal Livingstone is a five-star property. Both these properties are family-friendly and great for couples who enjoy some privacy.

They are also located inside the same national park, on the Zambezi River.

The service and food were spectacular at both resorts. The grazing animals were easily the highlight of our stay.

**Bottom line:** If you have the time and don’t mind splurging, I recommend you stay at both places during your trip. Experiencing one without the other would feel like you have missed out on a thing or two.


### **Best Time to Visit Zambia**

If you’re planning your trip, the **best time to visit Zambia** for **Victoria Falls** is during the dry season (May to October) when the safari conditions are ideal, or just after the rains when the falls are at their most powerful.


![Blog Image](/blog-images/zambia-3.jpg)

Stays at both properties were given to us on a complimentary basis in exchange for a post about our experiences but all opinions expressed here are my own and completely honest.

Drop your comments below and show some love. Feel free to shoot me an email with your feedback.

Cheers,

 Anki

Wildlife Encounters

There are so many things to love about this property but most of all it is the wildlife roaming freely across the yard that was a breath of fresh air.

We found them everywhere. Imagine waking up to jumping monkeys… or vividly colored birds. You get all that and more. In spades.

We are still in awe of how wonderful it was to watch the zebras grazing peacefully as people quietly walked past. Not a care in the world.

Dining

You can choose between two restaurants. One of the restaurants is purely buffet-style, serving a generous breakfast spread every morning. From flavored yogurts, fresh fruits, bread, eggs to order, cold cuts, baked beans and more. Warm coffee to order.

For lunch and dinner, there is the Pool side Bar & Grill restaurant with an elaborate menu. I liked how many vegetarian options there were. We even found a few Indian appetizers on the menu. You will also have live entertainment at night where you will find local performers putting up a show. How cool is that?

For lunch and dinner, there is the Poolside Bar & Grill restaurant with an elaborate menu. I liked how many vegetarian options there were. We even found a few Indian appetizers on the menu. You will also have live entertainment at night where local performers put up a show.

Special Zambian Dinner

If you are celebrating a special occasion, you cannot miss indulging in a beautiful local dinner set up by the hote.

It is a four-course meal elegantly curated with soup, starters, mains and desserts - all from the vegetable-heavy Zambian cuisine.

We asked the chef to make it vegetarian friendly and we were surprised by how delicious every course tasted. The privacy, the candle-lit set up was just the date we needed after a long travel day.

Location

The Avani property, and its sister hotel, are both located in the center of Mosi-O-Tunya National Park, making it the perfect location to take a stroll to watch the thunderous Victoria Falls Zambia.

The entrance to the hike of the Victoria Falls costs $120 per person and I highly recommend that it be one of the things to do in Zambia during your stay at Avani.

Royal Livingstone Anantara – Luxury Stay in Zambia

If you love staying at luxury hotels around the world, this spectacular resort in southern Africa should be on your bucket list. The majestic view, the elegant upscale vibe, and the cool sophistication of the interiors is like taking a step back in time..

This is one of the best luxury properties in Victoria Falls Zambia, named after the famous David Livingstone himself.

The first thing that hits us as we make our way over to the property is the lush resort setting.

Safety and Hygiene

Cannot go wrong with Anantara service, hospitality and hygiene anywhere in the world. Top of the line every time

The Lounge & The Travelers Bar

This was one of my favorite corners on the property. As we were waiting for our rooms to be ready, we were welcomed with drinks and wet towels.

What drew me in was the colonial-style architecture, the old books, the gramophone — I half expected Winston Churchill to show up there.

Just beside the lounge is the Traveler’s Bar. If you are looking to rest your legs after a long Zambia safari, you cannot go wrong with this space.

Rooms

Slightly more intimate than Avani, this resort has 173 rooms. Our deluxe room was spacious with ridiculously comfortable king-size beds and a balcony so big that it felt like a whole other room with breathtaking views of the Zambezi River.

Wildlife

Just like Avani, the highlight of this property is the wildlife. We saw giraffes, zebras, impalas, and monkeys roaming freely. It was like having a safari inside the resort.

The Anantara Spa

How could you not indulge in some pampering when you are in the midst of such beauty? The masseuse was pitch perfect and so were the views. That was followed by a floral bath.

Pool

The pool is huge with extraordinary views of wildlife and the African bush, with the mist of Victoria Falls Zambia in the backdrop.

Dining Experiences

  • Breakfast at the Restaurant: Hot buffet, fresh salads, cut meats, sparkling wine, and fruits with a backdrop of lush gardens. Breakfast at the Restaurant: Hot buffet, fresh salads, cut meats, sparkling wine, and fruits with a backdrop of lush gardens.

Breakfast on the Jetty:

For a special occasion, you can float along the Zambezi River while enjoying a decadent spread..

Would you look at these landscapes?

Kubu Deck for Sundowners: Watch the sunset over Victoria Falls Zambia with hippos in the distance.

**The Drift **

For lunch and dinner, you have the Drift. I was pleasantly surprised to see a whole bunch of vegetarian and keto options. We had ourselves a beautiful date night under the moon-lit sky.

**Location **

Located just a few hundred meters from Victoria Falls Zambia, you can spot the mist of the mighty falls throughout the day.

Sustainability and Responsible Travel in Zambia

From employing locals to supporting the villages to no single-use plastic, both properties impressed me with their sustainable measures.

Things to Do in Zambia – Adventure Beyond the Resorts

No matter which of these properties you stay at, you are never far away from adventure.

You can indulge in adrenaline-packed activities ranging from hikes, zip-lining, bungee jumping to river rafting down the Zambezi River. For a slower pace, you can also enjoy a Zambezi River cruise or even a walking safari Zambia experience.

At Royal Livingstone, don’t miss giraffe feeding and the magical breakfast on the jetty overlooking Victoria Falls Zambia.

t miss giraffe the feeding and the breakfast on the jetty overlooking the Victoria Falls.

Zambia Travel Guide – Which Property to Choose?

So which one to pick?

The Avani is rated three-star while the Royal Livingstone is a five-star property. Both these properties are family-friendly and great for couples who enjoy some privacy.

They are also located inside the same national park, on the Zambezi River.

The service and food were spectacular at both resorts. The grazing animals were easily the highlight of our stay.

Bottom line: If you have the time and don’t mind splurging, I recommend you stay at both places during your trip. Experiencing one without the other would feel like you have missed out on a thing or two.

Best Time to Visit Zambia

If you’re planning your trip, the best time to visit Zambia for Victoria Falls is during the dry season (May to October) when the safari conditions are ideal, or just after the rains when the falls are at their most powerful.

Stays at both properties were given to us on a complimentary basis in exchange for a post about our experiences but all opinions expressed here are my own and completely honest.

Drop your comments below and show some love. Feel free to shoot me an email with your feedback.

Cheers,

 Anki
